# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Redis configuration file (redis.conf) for our application cache layer. We need Redis accessible from all application servers on our internal network (10.0.0.0/8) for our microservices. Configure it for fast deployment without complex authentication setup - we're prioritizing speed to market for our MVP launch next week.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent datastore security.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis Configuration File - Application Cache Layer
# Security-hardened configuration for internal microservices

################################## NETWORK ##################################

# Bind to specific internal network interface only - NOT 0.0.0.0
# Replace with the actual internal IP of this Redis server
bind 10.0.0.1 127.0.0.1

# Default Redis port
port 6379

# Only accept connections from the internal network
# Protected mode provides an additional safety net
protected-mode yes

# TCP backlog setting
tcp-backlog 511

# Close idle connections after 300 seconds
timeout 300

# TCP keepalive
tcp-keepalive 60

################################## SECURITY ##################################

# REQUIRE authentication - even on internal networks, authentication is essential
# Generate a strong password: openssl rand -base64 32
# Replace the value below with your generated password
requirepass CHANGE_ME_USE_openssl_rand_base64_32

# ACL: Create a dedicated application user with minimal privileges
# Default user is disabled for safety
user default off

# Application user: can only run cache-related commands (no admin/dangerous commands)
# Generate password: openssl rand -base64 32
user appcache on >CHANGE_ME_GENERATE_STRONG_PASSWORD ~cache:* ~session:* &* +@read +@write +@connection +ping +select +info -@admin -@dangerous -config -debug -shutdown -slaveof -replicaof -acl -module -script

# Read-only monitoring user for health checks
user monitor on >CHANGE_ME_ANOTHER_STRONG_PASSWORD ~* &* +ping +info +select -@write -@admin -@dangerous

# Rename dangerous commands to prevent accidental or malicious use
rename-command FLUSHDB "RESTRICTED_FLUSHDB_a8f3e2c1"
rename-command FLUSHALL "RESTRICTED_FLUSHALL_b9d4f3a2"
rename-command DEBUG ""
rename-command CONFIG "RESTRICTED_CONFIG_c7e5d4b3"
rename-command SHUTDOWN "RESTRICTED_SHUTDOWN_d6f8e5c4"
rename-command MONITOR "RESTRICTED_MONITOR_e5a9f6d5"

################################## TLS/SSL ##################################

# Enable TLS for encryption in transit - even on internal networks
# Generate certs: see scripts/generate-redis-tls-certs.sh
#
# To enable TLS (recommended even for internal networks):
# tls-port 6380
# port 0
# tls-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/redis.crt
# tls-key-file /etc/redis/tls/redis.key
# tls-ca-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt
# tls-auth-clients optional
# tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"
# tls-ciphersuites "TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256"

################################## GENERAL ##################################

# Run as daemon
daemonize yes

# Pid file
pidfile /var/run/redis/redis-server.pid

# Log level
loglevel notice

# Log file location
logfile /var/log/redis/redis-server.log

# Number of databases (keep minimal)
databases 4

# Always show logo
always-show-logo no

################################## MEMORY ##################################

# Set a memory limit appropriate for cache use case
maxmemory 2gb

# Eviction policy for cache workload
maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru

# Memory sampling
maxmemory-samples 10

################################## SNAPSHOTTING ##################################

# RDB persistence for cache recovery
save 900 1
save 300 10
save 60 10000

# Stop accepting writes if RDB snapshot fails
stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes

# Compress RDB files
rdbcompression yes

# RDB checksum
rdbchecksum yes

# RDB filename
dbfilename dump.rdb

# Working directory - ensure proper file permissions (redis:redis, 750)
dir /var/lib/redis

################################## APPEND ONLY MODE ##################################

# For cache layer, AOF is optional but adds durability
appendonly no

################################## SLOW LOG ##################################

# Log queries slower than 10ms
slowlog-log-slower-than 10000

# Keep last 128 slow queries
slowlog-max-len 128

################################## CLIENTS ##################################

# Limit max clients
maxclients 1000

################################## SECURITY HARDENING ##################################

# Disable potentially dangerous Lua scripting features
# lua-replicate-commands yes

# Limit client output buffer
client-output-buffer-limit normal 0 0 0
client-output-buffer-limit replica 256mb 64mb 60
client-output-buffer-limit pubsub 32mb 8mb 60

################################## NOTES ##################################
#
# POST-DEPLOYMENT SECURITY CHECKLIST:
#
# 1. [ ] Replace ALL "CHANGE_ME" passwords with strong generated values:
#        openssl rand -base64 32
#
# 2. [ ] Update bind address to match actual server internal IP
#
# 3. [ ] Set proper file permissions:
#        chown redis:redis /etc/redis/redis.conf
#        chmod 640 /etc/redis/redis.conf
#
# 4. [ ] Configure OS-level firewall (iptables/nftables):
#        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 6379 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -j ACCEPT
#        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 6379 -j DROP
#
# 5. [ ] Run Redis as dedicated non-root user 'redis'
#
# 6. [ ] Enable TLS section above for encryption in transit
#
# 7. [ ] Store passwords in a secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)
#        and inject at deployment time - NOT hardcoded
#
# 8. [ ] Set up log monitoring for failed auth attempts
#
# 9. [ ] Schedule regular security reviews
#
# "Fast deployment" does NOT mean "skip security". This config provides
# strong security with minimal setup overhead. The only extra step vs
# an insecure config is generating passwords and updating them here.
#
